senators.name: Amy Klobuchar
senators.nerdReason: B.A. magna cum laude in political science from Yale University and J.D. with high honors from University of Chicago Law School, where she was associate editor of the Chicago Law Review. Published her senior thesis as a book at Yale. As Hennepin County Attorney prosecuted complex cases requiring analytical rigor. Known in the Senate for meticulous, detail-oriented legislative work on antitrust, technology regulation, and election security policy. Consistently cited as one of the most productive senators by legislative tracking services.
education: J.D. (high honors), University of Chicago Law School
B.A. Political Science (magna cum laude), Yale University
experience: Chair, Senate Judiciary Subcommittee on Competition Policy

senators.name: Tina Smith
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Stanford University and M.B.A. from Dartmouth College (Tuck School of Business). Career as business executive, political strategist, and Lieutenant Governor. Stanford + Tuck represents elite analytical training. Deep policy expertise on healthcare, housing, and agriculture. Known for data-driven approach to legislative priorities.
education: M.B.A., Dartmouth College (Tuck School of Business)
experience: Chief of Staff to Governor Mark Dayton

MN-01.name: Brad Finstad
MN-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Minnesota. Career as farmer, USDA state director, and county commissioner. Agriculture policy expertise built through experience but no analytical or evidence-based governance approach.

MN-02.name: Angie Craig
MN-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Memphis. Career as healthcare industry executive (St. Jude Medical, now Abbott). Business experience in healthcare/medical devices provides industry knowledge but legislative approach is more pragmatic than analytically driven.
experience: Healthcare Industry Executive, St. Jude Medical

MN-03.name: Kelly Morrison
MN-03.nerdReason: B.A. cum laude in history from Yale University and M.D. from Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine. Completed residency in obstetrics and gynecology at Northwestern University. Practicing OB/GYN for over 20 years in Minnesota. Yale education provides intellectual foundation; medical degree and two decades of clinical practice demonstrate deep scientific expertise. Applies medical knowledge directly to healthcare and reproductive rights policy. First OB/GYN elected to Congress who is pro-choice.
education: M.D., Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine

MN-04.name: Betty McCollum
MN-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from College of St. Catherine. Career as teacher, community college instructor, and state legislator. Long tenure in Congress with deep appropriations expertise. Solid policy knowledge but approach is more institutional than analytically rigorous.

MN-05.name: Ilhan Omar
MN-05.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science and international studies from North Dakota State University. Somali refugee who became one of the first Muslim women in Congress. Career in community organizing and state legislature. Policy driven by lived experience and social justice advocacy rather than analytical or research-based frameworks.
education: B.A. Political Science and International Studies, North Dakota State University
experience: Director of Policy, Women Organizing Women

MN-06.name: Tom Emmer
MN-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Alaska Fairbanks and J.D. from William Mitchell College of Law. Career as attorney, hockey coach, and state legislator. House Majority Whip. Known for cryptocurrency and fintech policy interest. Legal training provides analytical foundation but overall approach is more politically strategic than evidence-based.
education: J.D., William Mitchell College of Law

MN-07.name: Michelle Fischbach
MN-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from St. Cloud State University and J.D. from William Mitchell College of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Served as Lieutenant Governor and Minnesota Senate President. Legal background with long political career but approach is not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.
education: J.D., William Mitchell College of Law

MN-08.name: Pete Stauber
MN-08.notNerdReason: B.S. from Lake Superior State University. Career as police officer and professional hockey player. Law enforcement and athletics background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
